patient.name: patient
patient.notes: Patient is 5 weeks post-op ACL surgery on the right knee. Pain level 5/10. Has previous PT experience. Focus on graft protection and progressive strengthening for return to sport.

plan.phase_description: This initial phase (weeks 5-9 post-op) focuses on reducing pain and swelling, restoring full knee extension and improving flexion, and beginning gentle strengthening of the quadriceps and hamstrings to protect the new graft. We'll gradually build strength and prepare for more advanced movements.

weeks.focus: Restoring full extension, gentle quad activation, pain management

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Perform slowly, focusing on engaging the quad. Keep the knee straight.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Use a rolled towel or foam roller under the ankle to allow the heel to hang and achieve full extension. Gently press the knee down.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Ensure no pain during movement. If pain increases, reduce range or stop.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Hold the full extension for 3-5 seconds at the end of each rep.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Focus on controlled movement both up and down.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Progress to holding the contraction for 5 seconds.

weeks.focus: Increasing quad strength, initiating controlled knee flexion, improving balance

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Can add a light ankle weight (0.5-1kg) if pain-free.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Focus on a strong quad contraction at the top.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Start with a very shallow squat (15-30 degrees knee bend). Use a chair for support if needed. Ensure knees track over toes.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Maintain good form throughout. Avoid arching the back.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Ensure full extension with each rep.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Gradually increase depth as tolerated, but stay within a pain-free range (max 45-60 degrees).

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: If using weight, focus on slow and controlled lowering.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Focus on the burn in the quadriceps at the end of each set.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Maintain good posture and core engagement. Avoid letting knees collapse inward.

weeks.focus: Progressive strengthening of quads and hamstrings, improving squat depth

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Increase ankle weight if previous reps were easy and pain-free.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Focus on explosive contraction at the top, controlled release.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Aim for a deeper squat (up to 60-70 degrees) if comfortable and pain-free. Maintain control.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Perform lying on your stomach, gently curling the heel towards the glutes. Start with no weight, focus on controlled movement. Only if hamstring graft was NOT used, or very gently if it was (check with your surgeon/PT).

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Continue to challenge with weight if appropriate.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Ensure full lockout and hold.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Focus on consistency in depth and form. Can try single-leg mini-squat with support if stable and pain-free.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Ensure smooth movement, avoid jerking. Listen to your body.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Increase volume slightly.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Focus on the mind-muscle connection with the quad.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: If performing single-leg, maintain good balance and control.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Increase reps if no pain and good control.

weeks.focus: Building strength and endurance, preparing for next phase of rehab, functional movement

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Continue to increase resistance gradually if possible and pain-free.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Can incorporate a resistance band for added challenge.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Focus on controlled descent and powerful ascent. Can hold a light weight.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Can add light ankle weights if tolerated.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: Maintain perfect form even with added challenge.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Focus on endurance, minimal rest between sets if tolerated.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Increase sets for endurance. Focus on smooth movement patterns.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Increase reps or resistance if comfortable.

exercises.name: straight_leg_raise
exercises.notes: This is a foundational movement, continue to master it.

exercises.name: terminal_knee_extension
exercises.notes: Ensure full activation of the quadriceps.

exercises.name: mini_squat
exercises.notes: Vary stance slightly if comfortable to work different muscle fibers.

exercises.name: hamstring_curl
exercises.notes: Focus on the eccentric (lowering) phase for increased muscle building.
